name: openclaw-nerve
description: Real-time web cockpit for OpenClaw: voice conversations, agent automated kanban board, workspace/file control, sub-agent sessions, inline charts, and usage visibility.

commit.message:
fix(file-browser): evict invalid paths from tree cache (#105)

* fix(file-browser): evict invalid paths from tree cache

- Remove 400/404 paths from expandedPaths cache when API returns errors
- Preserve cache for transient errors (500, network) to allow retries
- Add comprehensive test coverage for cache eviction behavior
- Fix localStorage persistence assertion and test title accuracy
- No backend changes required - uses existing error responses

* fix(file-browser): handle permanent tree cache errors

commit.message:
fix(ws): prevent infinite reconnect loop on auth failure

On auth rejection (e.g. rotated gateway token), set intentionalDisconnectRef
before closing the socket so onclose won't schedule another retry.

Also removes the unreachable RECONNECT_MAX_ATTEMPTS guard (dead code since
the limit was set to Infinity).

fix: prevent inotify exhaustion; improve WS reconnect + subagent visibility (#102)

* fix(watcher): disable recursive workspace watch by default

* fix(realtime): keep WS reconnecting; widen sessions.list for subagent visibility

- Add mobile-responsive file browser that collapses on small screens
- Implement controlled component pattern with localStorage persistence
- Add expand button in chat header for mobile access
- Include comprehensive test coverage for mobile/desktop behavior

- Make onCollapseChange required when collapsed prop is provided
- Remove optional chaining to prevent no-op toggle UI
- Simplify toggleCollapsed function implementation

commit.message:
feat: live transcription preview in chat input

Surface interim SpeechRecognition transcript into the textarea during
voice recording, giving real-time visual feedback of what's being
recognized. The preview text is styled as provisional (italic, 50%
opacity) and cleared when recording stops or final transcription arrives.

Changes:
- useVoiceInput: add interimTranscript state, populate from onresult
  handler in stop mode, clear on discard/stop, return from hook
- InputBar: sync interimTranscript to uncontrolled textarea via
  useEffect, apply provisional styling during recording

fix(voice): audio playback quality improvements (#74)

- Switch from HTMLAudioElement to AudioContext + AudioBuffer for glitch-free playback
- Preload all sound effects into memory on page load
- Convert send/cancel/notify to OGG/Opus (no MP3 encoder delay artifacts)
- Replace wake sound with clean ElevenLabs file, trim trailing silence
- Add 500ms delay before mic acquisition after wake word to prevent audio disruption
- Guard against double wake word trigger from interim + final speech recognition results
- Rewrite audio-feedback tests for AudioBuffer-based implementation

verification.payload:
tree b6e4b2dba03fef74701ad9396a464f52d802e2b7
parent 4d7acb08ec188ee43367a60709cd1783dc6c2828
author lil reentrant <cagindonmez@protonmail.ch> 1772502185 +0300
committer GitHub <noreply@github.com> 1772502185 +0300

fix(voice): audio playback quality improvements (#74)

- Switch from HTMLAudioElement to AudioContext + AudioBuffer for glitch-free playback
- Preload all sound effects into memory on page load
- Convert send/cancel/notify to OGG/Opus (no MP3 encoder delay artifacts)
- Replace wake sound with clean ElevenLabs file, trim trailing silence
- Add 500ms delay before mic acquisition after wake word to prevent audio disruption
- Guard against double wake word trigger from interim + final speech recognition results
- Rewrite audio-feedback tests for AudioBuffer-based implementation

commit.message:
fix(chat): render system notifications as collapsible strips (#69)

* fix(chat): render system notifications as collapsible strips (#kanban-backlog)

Subagent/cron completion messages were appearing as operator bubbles,
confusing users. Now they render as thin collapsible notification strips
with cyan accent, expandable to see full details.

- Added isSystemNotification/systemLabel to ChatMsg type
- detectSystemNotification() identifies gateway-injected messages
- Messages pass through filterMessage (no longer hidden) and get tagged
- MessageBubble renders tagged messages as collapsible strips
- 971/971 tests pass

* fix(lint): move useCallback before early return, remove unused import

* fix: address review feedback — memo comparator, status label, aria-expanded

- Add isSystemNotification/systemLabel to memo comparator to prevent stale renders
- Don't default unknown notification status to 'completed' — only show status when detected
- Add aria-expanded to system notification toggle button

verification.payload:
tree e040de174045e094108f3ccfaa17319dae7bf1d8
parent 9c90f971bc121be88142e15bd22394329be7bfe9
author lil reentrant <cagindonmez@protonmail.ch> 1772493842 +0300
committer GitHub <noreply@github.com> 1772493842 +0300

fix(chat): render system notifications as collapsible strips (#69)

* fix(chat): render system notifications as collapsible strips (#kanban-backlog)

Subagent/cron completion messages were appearing as operator bubbles,
confusing users. Now they render as thin collapsible notification strips
with cyan accent, expandable to see full details.

- Added isSystemNotification/systemLabel to ChatMsg type
- detectSystemNotification() identifies gateway-injected messages
- Messages pass through filterMessage (no longer hidden) and get tagged
- MessageBubble renders tagged messages as collapsible strips
- 971/971 tests pass

* fix(lint): move useCallback before early return, remove unused import

* fix: address review feedback — memo comparator, status label, aria-expanded

- Add isSystemNotification/systemLabel to memo comparator to prevent stale renders
- Don't default unknown notification status to 'completed' — only show status when detected
- Add aria-expanded to system notification toggle button

commit.message:
fix(kanban): use clean kb-{slug} as session display label

Keep timestamp in internal sessionKey for uniqueness, but use
kb-{id} as the spawn label so the AGENTS panel shows a clean name
without the timestamp suffix.

fix(chat): guard keydown handler during IME composition (#65) (#68)

CJK input methods fire keydown events during active composition.
Without a guard, Enter during composition triggers handleSend(),
causing the last syllable to be re-sent as a ghost message.

Add isComposing + keyCode 229 check at the top of handleKeyDown
to let the IME own all keystrokes during composition.

verification.payload:
tree c9acf07d471db1e9f642b581b856280a5dc0a9d6
parent 09246872a1c021e41f5e596998b911ca3d62bf1c
author lil reentrant <cagindonmez@protonmail.ch> 1772491937 +0300
committer GitHub <noreply@github.com> 1772491937 +0300

fix(chat): guard keydown handler during IME composition (#65) (#68)

CJK input methods fire keydown events during active composition.
Without a guard, Enter during composition triggers handleSend(),
causing the last syllable to be re-sent as a ghost message.

Add isComposing + keyCode 229 check at the top of handleKeyDown
to let the IME own all keystrokes during composition.

commit.message:
fix(ui): truncate long session names in agents panel

Add min-w-0 overflow-hidden to SessionInfoPanel trigger wrapper
to preserve the CSS text-ellipsis chain for long labels.

fix(kanban): human-readable task IDs and session labels (#67)

* fix(kanban): human-readable task IDs and session labels

- Task IDs now derived from title as slugs (max 30 chars) with dedup suffix
- Session keys use kb-{slug} instead of kanban-run-{uuid}-{timestamp}
- Fixes label truncation: gateway truncates labels >~50 chars, old format was 61 chars
- Fuzzy label matching with startsWith as safety net
- Poll timeout doubled to 60 min for long-running tasks
- recentMinutes: 120 on subagents list to catch completed sessions

* fix(kanban): address review feedback — tighten fuzzy matching, unique rerun keys, validate title

- Remove overly permissive bidirectional startsWith matching; keep only exact
  and ellipsis-truncated matching to prevent false positives (kb-fix-auth
  matching kb-fix-auth-leak)
- Session keys now include timestamp suffix (kb-{slug}-{ts}) so reruns of the
  same task get unique keys
- Validate payload.title in proposal-to-task path, fallback to 'untitled'
- Use task.run.sessionKey in routes instead of recomputing kb-{id}

* fix(test): stabilize listProposals ordering with delay between creates

* fix(kanban): remove fuzzy label matching — exact match only

New label format kb-{slug}-{timestamp} maxes out at ~47 chars,
well under the gateway's ~50 char truncation limit.

- Remove overly permissive bidirectional startsWith matching; keep only exact

  matching kb-fix-auth-leak)

commit.message: docs(readme): add kanban board to feature highlights and 'Why Nerve' section
docs: polish deployment guides — tighter copy, consistent formatting, better structure
docs(readme): add file ops to file browser, add auto-updater row
chore(release): bump version to 1.4.5

fix(kanban): remove hardcoded model fallback and add defaultThinking config

- Remove hardcoded anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-5 fallback from execute endpoint
- If no task-level or board-level model is set, omit the model param entirely
  so OpenClaw uses whatever default the operator configured
- Add defaultThinking to board config (schema, store type, execute logic)
- Thinking fallback chain: task.thinking > config.defaultThinking > omitted

fix(kanban): enable vertical scroll in board columns (#63)

Columns weren't scrolling when tasks overflowed. Three fixes:
- Add min-h-0 + flex-col to board body (flexbox min-height gotcha)
- Change board wrapper from flex-1 to h-full (DndContext breaks flex chain)
- Add h-full to columns and skeleton columns for height propagation

fix(kanban): enable vertical scroll in board columns (#63)

Columns weren't scrolling when tasks overflowed. Three fixes:
- Add min-h-0 + flex-col to board body (flexbox min-height gotcha)
- Change board wrapper from flex-1 to h-full (DndContext breaks flex chain)
- Add h-full to columns and skeleton columns for height propagation

commit.message:
fix(chat): repair broken memory collapse toggle behavior (#62)

* fix(chat): repair broken memory collapse toggle behavior

Fixes the relevant memories collapse/expand toggle which was broken. The toggle now properly expands and collapses on each click, with memories starting in collapsed state by design.

- Update memory collapse key derivation to use stable identifier (msgId/tempId) to match collapse state reading logic
- Update toggle logic to use nullish-coalescing semantics so undefined defaults are flipped correctly on first click

* refactor(chat): pass memory collapse key from ChatPanel

Compute relevant memories collapse key once in ChatPanel and pass it to MessageBubble to avoid duplicate derivation and future divergence.

committer GitHub <noreply@github.com> 1772388995 +0300

fix(chat): repair broken memory collapse toggle behavior (#62)

* fix(chat): repair broken memory collapse toggle behavior

Fixes the relevant memories collapse/expand toggle which was broken. The toggle now properly expands and collapses on each click, with memories starting in collapsed state by design.

- Update memory collapse key derivation to use stable identifier (msgId/tempId) to match collapse state reading logic
- Update toggle logic to use nullish-coalescing semantics so undefined defaults are flipped correctly on first click

* refactor(chat): pass memory collapse key from ChatPanel

Compute relevant memories collapse key once in ChatPanel and pass it to MessageBubble to avoid duplicate derivation and future divergence.

commit.message:
fix(tts): reset model when switching providers

Switching TTS provider (e.g. OpenAI → Replicate) kept the old
provider's model in localStorage, causing 400 errors when the
new provider didn't recognize it.

feat(ui): add gateway restart button to top bar (#49)

* feat(ui): add gateway restart button to top bar

Adds a POST /api/system/gateway-restart endpoint and a UI button to
restart the OpenClaw gateway service without dropping to the command line.

- server/routes/system.ts: new route, executes openclaw gateway restart,
  checks stdout for success string, returns ok/output; uses resolveOpenclawBin
  and rateLimitGeneral consistent with other routes
- server/app.ts: register systemRoutes
- src/components/TopBar.tsx: optional onGatewayRestart/gatewayRestarting
  props; RotateCw button with spin animation during restart
- src/App.tsx: confirmation dialog (variant=warning), inline notice banner
  (auto-dismisses after 6s) consistent with reconnecting banner pattern

* fix(gateway-restart): activating state, rate limit, banner UX

- Fix 'state activating' wrongly treated as failure during restart
- Add rateLimitRestart preset (3/min) instead of general (60/min)
- Add happy-path test (restart + status + port probe)
- Move restart notification from settings drawer to top banner
  (amber=restarting, green=success, red=failure)

---------

Co-authored-by: daggerhashimoto <cagindonmez@protonmail.ch>

commit.message:
feat: Task board with agent execution, drag-and-drop, and proposals (#61)

feat: Task board with agent execution, drag-and-drop, and proposals

- Full kanban board with 5 columns (Backlog, Todo, In Progress, Review, Done)
- Task CRUD with CAS versioning and optimistic conflict recovery
- Drag-and-drop reordering with dnd-kit (optimistic rollback on failure)
- Agent execution: spawn sessions via gateway, poll for completion, auto-move to review
- Workflow actions: execute, approve, reject, abort with notes
- Agent marker proposals: agents create/update tasks via output markers
- Proposal inbox with approve/reject popover
- Workspace quick view tab with click-to-open-board
- Board config: columns, default model, proposal policy
- Search, priority filters, stats chips
- Comprehensive docs: API, architecture, config, agent markers
- 221 tests (store, routes, parser)

commit.message:
fix(build): include server build in npm run build; simplify prod script (#47)

npm run build previously only built the frontend (tsc -b typechecks only
due to noEmit:true, then vite build). server-dist/ was never rebuilt,
so npm run start could silently serve stale server code.

Add npm run build:server to the build script so a single npm run build
produces a complete production-ready output (dist/ + server-dist/).

Also remove the now-redundant npm run build:server call from the prod
script — since build already runs it, prod was invoking build:server
twice. Simplify to: npm run build && node server-dist/index.js.

verification.payload:
tree edba327f36edfdefe4fad9b5b6803280bf01d89e
parent 12cc93a39b2b811abdc78bf76991a645afdfcf84
author James Martin <50209269+jamesjmartin@users.noreply.github.com> 1772344062 -0500
committer GitHub <noreply@github.com> 1772344062 +0300

fix(build): include server build in npm run build; simplify prod script (#47)

npm run build previously only built the frontend (tsc -b typechecks only
due to noEmit:true, then vite build). server-dist/ was never rebuilt,
so npm run start could silently serve stale server code.

Add npm run build:server to the build script so a single npm run build
produces a complete production-ready output (dist/ + server-dist/).

Also remove the now-redundant npm run build:server call from the prod
script — since build already runs it, prod was invoking build:server
twice. Simplify to: npm run build && node server-dist/index.js.

commit.message: fix(setup): allow cron and gateway tools in gateway.tools.allow (#17)
chore: bump version to 1.4.3
release: v1.4.3
docs: changelog v1.4.3, update architecture for hook split
merge: WS observability, keepalive pings, docs normalization, installer safety
merge: centralize config mutations, fix WS nonce race
merge: split ChatContext into hooks, fix AudioContext and STT sync

fix: detect port conflicts during install (closes #38)

Adds check_port() helper using ss/lsof/netstat fallback chain.
If default port 3080 is occupied:
- Non-interactive mode: fail with clear error
- Interactive mode: prompt user for an alternative port
fix: reset gatewayAlive on reconnect to prevent keepalive killing new socket
fix(ws-proxy): preserve connect-first ordering during nonce wait
fix(chat): stabilize subscription effect deps
fix: replace Promise<any> with Promise<unknown> in recovery hook
fix: remove unused connId param from createGatewayRelay

fix: remove as-any casts and unused import per review

- Replace `as any` with @ts-expect-error for negative-path tests
- Remove unused HttpServerType import (ws-proxy.test.ts)

fix: address review — remove unused imports, fix installer safety, reorder env keys

- Remove unused PONG_TIMEOUT constant (ws-proxy.ts)
- Remove unused randomUUID import (ws-proxy.test.ts)
- Reorder GATEWAY_TOKEN before GATEWAY_URL (.env.example)
- Check PID liveness before kill in cleanup()
- Capture wait exit status explicitly to avoid set -e trap

fix: memoize hook return values to prevent re-render cascades

All 4 extracted hooks (useChatMessages, useChatStreaming, useChatRecovery,
useChatTTS) now wrap their return objects in useMemo. Without this, every
render created new object references, causing useEffect dependencies in
ChatContext to fire continuously.

fix: add WS keepalive pings and revert sessionStorage change

- Server pings both client and gateway every 30s
- Terminates dead connections on pong timeout
- Reverts sessionStorage for gateway credentials (back to localStorage)
fix: unify AudioContext, fix STT sync race, add voice error states
docs: add implementation summary for ChatContext split
fix: resolve challenge-nonce timing race in WS proxy
fix: centralize config mutations with typed updateConfig()

refactor: split ChatContext into composable hooks

Extract 4 hooks from the 1,116-line ChatContext god object:
- useChatTTS: TTS playback, voice fallback, sound feedback
- useChatMessages: message CRUD, dedup, history, infinite scroll
- useChatStreaming: stream rendering, processing stage, activity log
- useChatRecovery: recovery/retry, gap detection, generation guards

ChatContext is now a 668-line thin orchestrator that composes hooks.
Context API shape unchanged — zero consumer modifications needed.

tsc: clean | vitest: 708/708 passed
fix: installer safety — trap cleanup, stderr capture, eval fix, build rollback
feat: add connection IDs and structured logging to WS proxy and SSE

docs: normalize config references and fix documentation drift

- Fix .env.example PORT from 3081 (dev value) to 3080 (production default)
- Add missing env vars to .env.example: NERVE_AUTH, NERVE_PASSWORD_HASH,
  NERVE_SESSION_SECRET, NERVE_SESSION_TTL, NERVE_ALLOW_INSECURE,
  EDGE_VOICE_GENDER, WHISPER_MODEL_DIR, CODEX_DIR, TRUSTED_PROXIES
- Add actual default values as comments in .env.example path vars
- Reorganize .env.example with section headers matching CONFIGURATION.md
- Add EDGE_VOICE_GENDER to CONFIGURATION.md STT table
- Add NERVE_ALLOW_INSECURE to CONFIGURATION.md Authentication section
- Add API Base URLs section (OPENAI_BASE_URL, REPLICATE_BASE_URL)
- Add CODEX_DIR to CONFIGURATION.md
- Fix trailing slash inconsistency on WHISPER_MODEL_DIR default
- Update full .env example with auth vars, speech settings, path fixes
fix: use sessionStorage for gateway credentials per security comment

commit.message:
fix: add happy-path auth test, sync comment on voice regex

CodeRabbit findings: added valid-password login test with scrypt hash,
clarified regex sync comment in voice-fallback test.

fix: remove WS proxy path restriction (compatibility risk)

Hostname allowlist + protocol check already provide the security boundary.
Path restriction could break setups with reverse proxy path prefixes.
Port range validation kept (pure sanity check).

fix: security hardening — auth, rate limiting, WS proxy, TTS fallback

Wave 1 from codebase review (all P0 items):

- Remove gateway token as login password fallback (timing-unsafe plaintext comparison)
- Add rateLimitAuth (5/min) for login endpoint (was 60/min via rateLimitGeneral)
- Refuse to start with HOST=0.0.0.0 and auth disabled (override: NERVE_ALLOW_INSECURE=true)
- Validate WS proxy target port range and restrict path to / and /ws* prefix
- Fix TTS fallback for non-Latin scripts: /[a-zA-Z]{3,}/ → /\p{L}{3,}/u
- Remove dead config branch (unreachable after process.exit guard)
- Add 15 new tests (708 total, was 693)

fix: properly test Authorization header with configured token

Split the auth header test into two: one verifying Content-Type is always
present, and one that mocks a gateway token via vi.doMock to verify the
Bearer header is included. The original test claimed to test token auth
but never configured a token.

fix: resolve CI build errors (tsc type checks)

- Add type assertions for res.json() in auth.test.ts (TS18046)
- Switch vi.mock(import(...)) to vi.mock(string) with typed importOriginal
  in gateway.test.ts and skills.test.ts (TS2769 execFile.__promisify__)
- Retain default export in child_process mock for vitest module resolution

fix: resolve CI lint errors in test files

- Remove unused _params from createDeviceBlock mock (ws-proxy.test.ts)
- Remove unused WS_ALLOWED_HOSTS import (ws-proxy.test.ts)
- Remove unused waitForOpen helper (ws-proxy.test.ts)
- Remove unused _req param and IncomingMessage import (mock-gateway.ts)

fix: address CodeRabbit review feedback on test suite

- Assert Authorization header in gateway-client test
- Remove unused imports (afterEach in auth, beforeEach in skills, vi in useInputHistory)
- Fix ContextMeter tests: assert token value and warning icon absence
- Remove unused 'cleaned' destructure in extractCharts test
- Use distinct object instances in mergeRecoveredTail dedup test
- Add negative assertion for replaced content in anchor path test
- Replace unsafe 'as any' cast with Record<string, unknown> in streamEventHandler
- Rename unused _req parameter in mock-gateway connection handler
- Use it.each for image extension tests (better failure reporting)
- Clarify bare URL regex comment in extractImages
- Strengthen empty content assertion in MarkdownRenderer
- Use path.isAbsolute() for cross-platform config path checks
- Wrap DOM mutation in try/finally in keyboard shortcuts test
- Assert gateway tool invocation args in session-info test
chore: gitignore .test-tasks/
style(tests): deslop AI-generated test code to match project conventions
test: validation round 3 — quality review passed, 3/3 green, no changes needed

releases.name: v1.4.8
releases.body:
## Highlights

**Voice input overhauled** — Free voice input modes improved with better finalization, shortened wake/send chimes, and reduced mic delay from 800ms to 370ms to stop clipping the first word. English phrase fallback no longer bleeds into non-English sessions.

**Kanban skill bundled** — `nerve-kanban` skill now auto-installs during setup. Agents can use the kanban skill to manage the Nerve task board directly: create tasks, move columns, update status, all through natural conversation.

## Added
- Bundled `nerve-kanban` skill with auto-install during setup (#83)
- Improved free voice input modes and finalization (#80)

## Fixed
- File browser no longer overwrites dirty editor content when re-opening an already-open file (#85)
- Infinite scroll no longer stalls after loading older messages (#86)
- Chat message sends use atomic state updates to prevent race conditions with streaming events (#86)
- `groupToolMessages` and `mergeFinalMessages` no longer mutate shared React state (#86)
- Stale WebSocket `onclose` handlers no longer kill active connections during reconnect (#87)
- TTS voice flag resets on session switch, preventing phantom auto-speak (#88)
- TTS config fetch/save checks response status before parsing JSON (#88)
- TTS audio fetch includes credentials for cookie-based auth (#88)
- Voice phrase editor uses stable keys, fixing stale input on delete (#88)
- ConfirmDialog Enter key no longer fires confirm when Cancel is focused (#89)
- Dockerfile and Makefile syntax highlighting works in file browser (#89)
- Theme switch no longer reloads highlight.js stylesheet redundantly (#89)
- Updater rollback completes before releasing the lock (#90)
- `.env` parser strips surrounding quotes from values (#90)
- Image compression rejects oversized output instead of silently exceeding WS payload limit (#90)
- Kanban drag-and-drop no longer crashes on concurrent task deletion (#90)
- Duplicate task execution rejected with 409 instead of spawning a second agent session (#90)
- Shortened wake and send voice chimes and reduced mic delay from 800ms to 370ms (#91)
- File browser wrapper has proper height constraint for vertical scroll (#84)
- Kanban config migration backfills missing fields (#82)
- English phrase fallback no longer merges into non-English sessions (#81)

releases.name: v1.4.7
releases.body:
## v1.4.7

### Changed
- Added a **Live Transcription Preview** toggle in Audio settings so browser interim transcript rendering can be enabled/disabled per user (PR #78)
- Fresh defaults for local Whisper STT now use multilingual `base` across installer, server fallback, and fresh UI state (PR #78)
- Installer Whisper bootstrap now resolves and normalizes `WHISPER_MODEL` from `.env` (supports quotes/comments/aliases like `tiny`, `base`, `small` and `.en` variants) (PR #78)

### Fixed
- Edge TTS voice now auto-switches on language change and validates language-compatible voice overrides to prevent language/voice mismatch (PR #78)
- English custom `en-*` Edge voice overrides are preserved during auto-reconcile; server-side English override detection is now gender-aware (PR #78)
- Local Whisper model management now cancels stale model downloads and syncs active server model state on startup (PR #79)

**Full Changelog:** https://github.com/daggerhashimoto/openclaw-nerve/compare/v1.4.6...v1.4.7

releases.name: v1.4.6
releases.body:
### Added
- Live interim transcription preview in chat input during voice recording (PR #75)
- OpenAI TTS voice options expanded to all 13 supported voices (PR #72)
- Voice interaction sounds upgraded from oscillator beeps to custom MP3 effects (PR #70)

### Changed
- System notifications in chat now render as collapsible strips (PR #69)
- Kanban task IDs and session labels are now human-readable (PR #67)

### Fixed
- Voice audio playback quality improvements (PR #74)
- Chat panel remains mounted during tab switches to avoid voice/session disruption (PR #71)
- Chat keydown handling now safely guards IME composition input (PR #68)

---

releases.name: v1.4.5
releases.body:
## What's New

### Features
- **Task board** with full kanban workflow: drag-and-drop, agent execution, proposals, SSE live updates, board configuration, and audit log (#61)
- **Gateway restart button** in the top bar for one-click gateway restarts (#49 by @jamesjmartin)
- **File browser operations**: rename, move, trash, and restore files from the workspace panel (#44)
- Deployment guides for three topology scenarios: localhost, LAN/tailnet, and public cloud (#60)
- Updater now resolves the latest published GitHub release instead of defaulting to master HEAD (#45)

### Fixes
- Server build (`build:server`) now included in `npm run build`; `npm run prod` runs both builds (#47 by @jamesjmartin)
- Memory expand toggle: memory chat bubbles using mem0 plugin expand toggle no longer silently ignored (#62 by @jamesjmartin)
- Kanban board columns scroll vertically when tasks overflow viewport (#63)
- Switching TTS provider no longer sends the previous provider's model ID

### Contributors
Thanks to **@jamesjmartin** for three PRs this release: build fix (#47), gateway restart button (#49), and memory toggle fix (#62) 🎉

releases.name: v1.4.4
releases.body:
Focused around fixes for OpenClaw 2026.2.26 compatibility

## What's Changed
* fix(setup): allow cron + gateway tools for HTTP invoke policy by @daggerhashimoto in https://github.com/daggerhashimoto/openclaw-nerve/pull/40
* fix(skills): fail loud on CLI errors and parse warning-prefixed JSON by @daggerhashimoto in https://github.com/daggerhashimoto/openclaw-nerve/pull/41
* fix(setup): align pre-pair metadata with webchat identity by @daggerhashimoto in https://github.com/daggerhashimoto/openclaw-nerve/pull/42


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/daggerhashimoto/openclaw-nerve/compare/v1.4.3...v1.4.4

readme_html:
Nerve

The cockpit for your OpenClaw agents.

curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/daggerhashimoto/openclaw-nerve/master/install.sh | bash

What is Nerve?

You can already chat with your OpenClaw agent through webchat, Telegram, WhatsApp, Discord. Nerve is what you open when chatting isn't enough.

Nerve is a self-hosted web UI for OpenClaw AI agents. Voice conversations, live workspace editing, inline charts, cron scheduling, and full token-level visibility. One install script. Running in 30 seconds.

Why Nerve?

Messaging channels are great for chatting. But you can't watch charts render in real-time, edit your agent's workspace mid-conversation, browse its files, manage tasks on a kanban board, or monitor token spend from a Telegram window. Nerve is the dashboard that gives you the full picture.

What makes it different

Voice that actually works

Talk to your agent in 12 languages. Explicit language selection (no flaky auto-detect), wake-word activation, per-language stop/cancel phrases, and on-device Whisper transcription with model selection (tiny, base, small) plus GPU detection. No API key needed. Multi-provider TTS with Edge, OpenAI, and Replicate.

Full workspace visibility

Your sub-agent sessions, workspace files, memory, config, tools. All visible, all editable, all live. No file hunting, no guessing what it remembers.

Kanban task board

Drag-and-drop task management with agent execution, review workflow, and proposal inbox. Create tasks, assign them to your agent, and watch the work happen — all from a visual board.

Responsive by default

Nerve adapts cleanly across desktop, tablet, and mobile, with touch-friendly controls on smaller screens and no core workflow loss.

Live charts from a chat message

Your agent can drop interactive TradingView charts, candlestick plots, and data visualizations directly into the conversation. Say "show me gold this year" and get a real chart, not a code block.

Cron and scheduling from the UI

Create recurring jobs and one-shot reminders. Every scheduled run shows up as its own session in the sidebar. You can watch it execute live, read the full transcript, and see exactly what it did.

Everything else
Voice I/O Push-to-talk + wake word, live transcription preview, language-aware stop/cancel phrases, local Whisper model picker, TTS providers (Edge/OpenAI/Replicate)
Streaming chat Markdown, syntax highlighting, diff views, image paste, file previews. All rendering as it streams
File browser Browse your workspace, rename, move, trash, and restore files. Open files in tabs. Support for custom workspace roots via FILE_BROWSER_ROOT
Built-in editor CodeMirror editor with syntax highlighting, conflict-safe saves, and automatic lock protection during concurrent agent edits
Multi-session Session tree with sub-agents, per-session model overrides, unread indicators
Kanban board Drag-and-drop task management with agent execution, review workflow, and proposal inbox
Responsive UI Fully responsive layout across desktop, tablet, and mobile with touch-friendly controls
Sub-agents Spawn background workers with custom models and reasoning levels
Monitoring Token usage, context window meter, cost tracking, activity logs
Command palette Cmd+K to search, switch sessions, change models. Keyboard-first
Search Full-text search across all messages in the current session
Images Paste from clipboard, drag & drop files, full-screen lightbox with download
Skills browser Browse installed skills, check status and requirements from the workspace panel
Local STT On-device Whisper — tiny, base, or small models with multilingual support, explicit language selection, GPU detection, and auto-download. No API key needed
Code actions Copy or save-to-file buttons on every code block
API key management Add provider keys from settings — writes to .env and hot-reloads, no restart needed
14 themes Dark, light, and everything in between. Resizable panels, custom fonts
Auto-updater Built-in updater with automatic rollback. One command to update, verify, and restart

Get Started

One command

curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/daggerhashimoto/openclaw-nerve/master/install.sh | bash

The installer handles dependencies, cloning, building, and launching. It runs a setup wizard that auto-detects your gateway token and walks you through configuration.

Pick your setup

  • Run everything on one machine
  • Use a cloud Gateway with Nerve on your laptop
  • Run both Nerve and Gateway in the cloud

Manual install

git clone https://github.com/daggerhashimoto/openclaw-nerve.git
cd openclaw-nerve
npm install
npm run setup    # interactive wizard — configures .env
npm run prod     # builds and starts the server

Updating

npm run update -- --yes

Fetches the latest release, rebuilds, restarts, and verifies health. Auto-rolls back on failure. See docs/UPDATING.md for flags and details.

Development

npm run dev            # frontend — Vite HMR on :3080
npm run dev:server     # backend — watch mode on :3081

Requires: Node.js 22+ and an OpenClaw gateway.

How it works

Browser ─── Nerve (:3080) ─── OpenClaw Gateway (:18789)
  │            │
  ├─ WS ──────┤  proxied to gateway
  ├─ SSE ─────┤  file watchers, real-time sync
  └─ REST ────┘  files, memories, TTS, models

Nerve proxies WebSocket traffic to your gateway and adds its own REST layer.

Frontend: React 19 · Tailwind CSS 4 · shadcn/ui · Vite 7 Backend: Hono 4 on Node.js

Security

Nerve binds to 127.0.0.1 (localhost) by default — only you can access it. When you expose it to the network (HOST=0.0.0.0), built-in password authentication protects all endpoints. The setup wizard auto-prompts for a password when network access is configured.

  • Session cookies — HttpOnly, SameSite=Strict, HMAC-SHA256 signed
  • Password storage — scrypt with 32-byte salt
  • WebSocket auth — cookie verified on upgrade
  • Gateway token fallback — works as a password with zero config

See Security for the full threat model.
